channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Amazon’s Alexa generative AI upgrade is finally coming after delays
item.description: Amazon is set to release its long-awaited—and delayed—Alexa generative artificial intelligence voice service, said three people familiar with the matter, and has scheduled a press event for later this month to preview it.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: DeepSeek's Ties to China Mobile Raise National Security Concerns
item.description: Security researchers have found computer code on DeepSeek's website that links the chatbot to China Mobile, a state-owned telecommunications company barred from operating in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Man jailed over police AI program, then freed 17 months after victim raised doubts
item.description: A grainy surveillance photo of an assault suspect on St. Louis' Metrolink led a computer facial recognition program to identify Chris Gatlin.
item.dc:creator: Chris Hayes, NewsNation

item.title: Workday to cut 1,750 jobs, shift focus to AI and global expansion
item.description: Workday said Wednesday it will lay off 1,750 employees, roughly 8.5% of its workforce, as part of a restructuring plan to invest more heavily in artificial intelligence (AI) and accelerate international growth.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: EXL’s Insurance LLM transforms claims and underwriting
item.description: As insurance companies embrace generative AI (genAI) to address longstanding operational inefficiencies, they’re discovering that general-purpose large language models (LLMs) often fall short in solving their unique challenges.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: East Bay tech company eyes 1,000-plus global layoffs, office space cuts
item.description: A big East Bay tech company warned on Wednesday that it plans to slash well over 1,000 jobs even as it pushes into AI opportunities.
item.dc:creator: George Avalos, The Mercury News

item.title: Why this cybersecurity startup wants to watermark everything
item.description: Cybersecurity startup EchoMark is releasing a new application programming interface (API) to allow for its novel digital watermarking tool to integrate with virtually any existing communications software.
item.dc:creator: Jared Keller, Fast Company

item.title: Patients using diabetes apps can miss critical alerts. Here's how to make sure you're getting them
item.description: U.S. officials are warning about a potentially life-threatening issue with smartphone apps that help manage diabetes.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Perrone, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: With ‘Air,’ Opera launches a browser to reduce stress
item.description: If you get stressed out when surfing the web, Norwegian browser company Opera has something for you: a new browser for Windows and Mac designed to help you relax. Opera Air offers a minimalist design and scheduled breaks for breathing exercises, neck training and meditation, among other things.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Google's rising holiday's season ad sales aren't enough to ease worries about AI letdown
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Google's digital ad sales continued to grow at a healthy clip during the holiday season, but that wasn't enough to offset investors' worries about whether its big bet on artificial intelligence will be lucrative as once envisioned.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge says Elon Musk's claims of harm from OpenAI are a 'stretch' but welcomes possible trial
item.description: Elon Musk’s lawyers faced off with OpenAI in court Tuesday as a federal judge weighed the billionaire’s request for a court order that would block the ChatGPT maker from converting itself to a for-profit company.
item.dc:creator: Barbara Ortutay And Matt O'Brien, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Hawley makes push to allow parents to sue tech companies
item.description: Sen. Josh Hawley (R-Mo.) said Tuesday he will introduce legislation to empower parents to sue leading social media companies over alleged harms to children and teens.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Meta promises it won’t release dangerous AI systems
item.description: According to a new policy document from Meta, the Frontier AI Framework, the company might not release AI systems developed in-house in certain risky scenarios. The document defines two types of AI systems that can be classified as either “high risk” or “critical risk.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Bill Gates: Musk wouldn't be telling USAID workers to stay home if he knew what it does
item.description: Billionaire Bill Gates said in an interview early Tuesday that Elon Musk "wouldn't be telling" employees at the U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID) not to come to work if he "really knew" what it does. “I give billions of dollars to the same thing that USAID does.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: How an ancient asteroid strike carved out 2 grand canyons on the moon
item.description: New research shows that when an asteroid slammed into the moon billions of years ago, it carved out a pair of grand canyons on the far side.
item.dc:creator: Marcia Dunn, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: How to easily transfer files and photos off a computer to your hard drive
item.description: It's not just about freeing up space on your computer, Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says. It's about protecting your digital memories and important documents.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: AMD patches microcode security holes after accidental early disclosure
item.description: AMD on Monday issued two patches for severe microcode security flaws, defects that AMD said “could lead to the loss of Secure Encrypted Virtualization (SEV) protection.” The bugs were inadvertently revealed by a partner last week.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Linux Foundation warns of US OFAC sanctions
item.description: Developers must be cautious about who they interact with and where contributions come from, warns a Linux Foundation blog post intended to help open-source developers navigate US OFAC (Office of Foreign Assets Control) sanctions.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Google makes its appeal to overturn jury verdict branding the Play Store as illegal monopoly
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Google went to appeals court Monday in an attempt to convince a three-judge panel to overturn a jury's verdict declaring its app store for Android smartphones as an illegal monopoly and block the penalties imposed by a federal judge to stop the misbehavior.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Canada's most populous province to pause retaliatory measures as US puts tariffs on hold
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — The leader of Ontario, Canada’s most populous province, said Monday he will pause all retaliatory measures against the United States after news broke that the threat of tariffs has been put on hold for a month.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: CISA hires former DHS CIO into top cyber position
item.description: Karen Evans is returning to the Department of Homeland Security in a top cyber job as DHS responds to major hacks into U.S. critical infrastructure. The post CISA hires former DHS CIO into top cyber position first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: DeepSeek
item.description: DeepSeek, founded in 2023 by Liang Wenfeng, a Chinese entrepreneur, engineer and former hedge fund manager, is generating a lot of buzz — and for good reason. Here are five things that make it stand out (as well as a listing of the latest news and analysis about DeepSeek).
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: OpenAI launches deep research agent for multi-step research tasks
item.description: Hot on the heels of its launch of the o3-mini model last week, OpenAI on Sunday announced another component for ChatGPT that allows the generative AI (genAI) tool to do more in-depth research.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Linux Foundation’s L3AF brings zero-downtime updates to eBPF network management
item.description: A challenge for enterprises that operate large-scale network infrastructure has long been how to maintain performance while updating critical systems.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Microsoft creates a new department to review how AI affects society
item.description: As part of Microsoft’s big push into AI services, the company has created a new department called the Advanced Planning Unit (APU). Employees of the APU will, among other things, study the effects of AI on society and make recommendations about which products are worth investing resources in.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Microsoft founder Bill Gates explores the making of his internal operating system in new memoir
item.description: As he prepares to turn 70 later this year, Microsoft founder Bill Gates' new memoir explores how his childhood quirks, upbringing, friendships and experiences coalesced into shaping his internal operating system.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Bill Gates shares his thoughts on vaccine backlash, Intel's woes and Google's antitrust battle
item.description: Although Microsoft founder Bill Gates sat down for an interview with The Associated Press to discuss his new memoir, “Source Code: My Beginnings,” he also shared his views on a variety of other topics, including vaccine conspiracy views about him, his thoughts on the struggles of longtime computer c
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: World's first AI-designed, 3D-printed shoe wants to be the next Crocs
item.description: A startup claims to have developed the world's first entirely artificial intelligence-designed and 3D-printed shoe, the brainchild of a 25-year-old.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What is NaaS? Providers, delivery models, and benefits explained
item.description: Network as a service (NaaS) is a cloud service model that’s designed to let enterprise IT professionals order network infrastructure components from a menu of options, have them configured to fit their business needs, and have the whole thing delivered, running and managed in a matter of hours inste
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: US cybersecurity agency's future role in elections remains murky under the Trump administration
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The nation’s cybersecurity agency has played a critical role in helping states shore up the defenses of their voting systems, but its election mission appears uncertain amid sustained criticism from Republicans and key figures in the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINA A. CASSIDY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Amazon sues state agency to block release of company records to Bezos-owned Washington Post
item.description: Amazon is suing a Washington state agency to prevent the release of some company materials to The Washington Post, the newspaper which is owned by Amazon founder Jeff Bezos.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC
